title: Enforce Specific 'run' Behavior for Classes Extending java.lang.Thread
description: Classes extending java.lang.Thread should always provide a specific 'run' behavior. The default implementation of java.lang.Thread 's run will only perform a task passed as a Runnable. If no Runnable has been provided at construction time, then the thread will not perform any action.
recommendation: When extending java.lang.Thread, override the run method or pass a Runnable target to the constructor of java.lang.Thread.
impact: Failing to provide a specific 'run' behavior for classes extending java.lang.Thread may lead to unpredictable behavior and bugs in the program.

checks.message: Classes extending Thread should override the run() method
